q: Acid-base reactions are essential in the fields of chemistry such as ___, agricultural chemistry, industrial chemistry, and environmental chemistry.
When an acid and a base react, they ___ each other to form a salt.
Antacids such as ___ and magnesium hydroxide (magnesia) are taken when a person has a stomach ulcer.
Calcium oxide (quicklime) is applied to ___ to neutralise it.
___ can be used to absorb harmful sulphur dioxide released from the power station.
___ is a measure of the acidity or alkalinity of the solution.
Buffering in the ___ is crucial to our survival.
The first person to define acids and bases was the Swedish chemist ___.
Acids are any substances that ___ to produce hydrogen ions ($H^{+}$) when they dissolve in water.
___ such as hydrochloric acid completely ionise in water.
___ such as ethanoic acid only partially ionise.
Bases are any substances that dissociate to produce ___ when they dissolve in water.
An example of an Arrhenius base is ___.
Arrhenius theory is unable to account for the ___ of $CO_{2}$, $SO_{2}$, and $SO_{3}$.
Arrhenius theory is unable to account for the ___ of $CaO$ and $MgO$.
The Danish chemist Johannes Nicolaus Brønsted and the English chemist Thomas Martin Lowry independently proposed the ___.
According to Brønsted-Lowry, acids are ___ and bases are proton acceptors.
An extension of the Brønsted-Lowry definition of acids and bases is the concept of the ___.
The ___ of a Brønsted-Lowry acid is the species that remains when one proton has been removed from the acid.
A conjugate acid results from the ___ to a Brønsted-Lowry base.
Every Brønsted-Lowry acid and base has a corresponding ___.
A strong acid has a ___ conjugate base.
The ethanoic acid molecule donates a proton to water forming the ___ ion.
The $H_{2}O$ molecule accepts a proton forming the $H_{3}O^{+}$ ion, known as the ___ ion.
Substances such as $BF_{3}$ and $AlCl_{3}$ do not have any hydrogen and thus cannot give a proton but are known to behave as ___.
In 1932, American chemist ___ proposed that bases are species that can donate an electron pair.
Acids are species that can ___ an electron pair.
The $NH_{3}$ molecule behaves as a ___ because it possesses a lone pair of electrons.
The reaction of ammonia with ___ is an acid-base reaction forming a Lewis salt.
